$comment: CANONICAL RIG CONTRACT — the single source of truth for the articulation
vocabulary shared between asset GENERATION (Kiln / pixel-forge) and TIJ
RUNTIME CONSUMERS. Loaded by scripts/import-war-catalog.ts.
Why this exists (and why it replaced per-asset rename lists): asset
generators emit internally-inconsistent joint names for the same physical
part (Kiln ships Joint_Rotor on the Huey but Joint_MainRotor on the OH-6;
Joint_PropLeft on the AC-47 but Joint_PropL on the OV-10; Joint_Gun on the
M48 but the consumer wants Joint_MainGun). The old rig-grafts.json carried a
hand-authored rename per asset, reverse-engineered from whatever the last
batch happened to produce — a treadmill that broke on every re-roll.
This file inverts that: the IMPORTER auto-detects every Joint_* node, maps
its generator-native name to ONE canonical name + semantic role via the
alias table below, renames the GLB node, and records {name,type,spinAxis} in
the generated catalog. Consumers read ONLY canonical names. A new batch that
renames a part again Just Works as long as the new name is added to that
role's `aliases` (a one-line vocabulary edit, not a per-asset graft).
spinAxis is role-constant and follows from two fixed facts: (1) the source
art is +X-forward (kiln/pixel-forge world convention) and (2) the importer
wraps every asset in a single +X->+Z axis-normalize rotation about Y. In the
joint's post-wrap local frame that makes a vertical rotor spin about Y, a
side-facing tail rotor about Z, and a forward-facing propeller about X. The
importer cross-checks each declared spinAxis against the joint's geometric
thin-axis and WARNS on disagreement, so a future frame change is caught at
import, not in a playtest.
CONSUMER CONTRACT (what reads these — keep in sync):
- HelicopterGeometry.wireRotorJoints: resolves catalog joints of
type mainBlades|tailBlades by name, stamps userData.type/spinAxis.
- FixedWingConfigs.catalogPropellers: filters catalog joints whose name
startsWith 'Joint_Propeller' -> propeller canonicals MUST keep that prefix.
- VehicleGlbVisuals.applyM48TankGlbVisual: getObjectByName('Joint_Turret')
+ getObjectByName('Joint_MainGun') (exact).
- WeaponRigManager: reads catalog magazineNodes/muzzleNodes mesh lists.

jointRoles.doc: Primary lifting rotor disc; spins about world-up.
Anti-torque tail rotor; vertical disc spinning about the lateral axis.
Forward rotor of a tandem-rotor helicopter (CH-47).
Aft rotor of a tandem-rotor helicopter (CH-47).
Port engine propeller (multi-engine prop aircraft). Name keeps the Joint_Propeller prefix the fixed-wing consumer filters on.
Rotating tank turret bulk. Re-seated on the TankTurret yaw rig by VehicleGlbVisuals; no procedural spin (slaved to crew aim).
Tank main gun mantlet + barrel. Re-seated on the TankTurret pitch rig; elevates with crew aim.

$comment: Mesh-name classification rules for weapon magazine + muzzle anchors,
applied to every weapon-class asset. Patterns are matched case-insensitively.
magazine = the detachable magazine BODY that drops on reload; the receiver
well and the release catch stay welded to the gun, so they are excluded.
muzzle = the forward muzzle device (flash hider / brake / suppressor / bore
opening); the bare barrel is NOT listed (WeaponRigManager already falls back
to Mesh_Barrel for the tip). `overrides` pins the handful the rules cannot
decide (e.g. the M79's only barrel mesh, or weapons with no muzzle device).
